summary: Blistering early‑'67 set with soaring Viola Lee Blues and epic Alligator>Caution
blurb: The show bursts open with a marathon Viola Lee Blues, then rolls through a powerful Alligator→Caution suite. Jerry’s guitar dominates while Pigpen’s organ/harp adds raw grit, and the dual‑drummer chemistry drives the energy.
review: Recorded on November 10, 1967 at the Shrine Exposition Hall, this early‑'67 Dead outing captures the band in a ferocious, blues‑heavy stride. The set kicks off with an astonishingly long (≈15‑minute) “Viola Lee Blues” that showcases Jerry Garcia’s fierce Les Paul tone and relentless soloing. From there the group builds momentum through Pigpen’s gritty vocal on “Good Morning Little School Girl,” a tight “Morning Dew,” and into the sprawling “Alligator → Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks)” suite, which many reviewers call the high‑water mark of the night. Dual drummers Billy Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart lock in with complementary power, while Phil Lesh’s bass walks boldly alongside Jerry’s lead lines. Pigpen’s organ and harmonica provide a raw, soulful counterpoint throughout, especially on “Good Morning Little School Girl” and the climactic “Caution.” Though one reviewer noted a minor tape glitch during “Viola Lee Blues,” the overall soundboard capture is crystal‑clear, making this performance a must‑listen for fans of the band’s early improvisational fire.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three of the four recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly praising Jerry’s ripping solos, Pigpen’s organ/harp presence, and the epic Alligator→Caution suite. One mixed review adds no criticism, so overall sentiment is strongly positive, justifying a +1.6 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with excellent balance, only a tiny hiss and a brief glitch; praised as the most pristine source for this performance.

key_highlights: 15‑minute “Viola Lee Blues” that puts Jerry’s guitar on full blast
Epic Alligator → Caution suite delivering the show’s high‑water mark
Dual‑drummer chemistry (Billy & Mickey) that drives the groove with power and nuance
Pigpen’s gritty organ/harp work adding raw blues authenticity

band_performance.Jerry: Fierce Les Paul tone and ripping, rapid‑fire solos dominate the set, especially on “Viola Lee Blues” and “Alligator.”
band_performance.Phil: Clear, aggressive walking bass that locks tightly with Jerry’s leads; shines in the multi‑track mix.
band_performance.Pigpen: Organ and harmonica work are gritty and powerful, holding his own through “Good Morning Little School Girl,” “Caution,” and other blues numbers.
band_performance.Mickey: Adds congas on “Alligator” and blends seamlessly with Billy’s drumming for a full, propulsive rhythm section.
band_performance.Billy: Provides tight, complementary drum work that drives the high‑energy flow of the set.
